CREATE TABLE networks (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, network INET);
SELECT n1.network FROM networks n1, networks n2 WHERE n1.network <<= n2.network;
SELECT n1.network FROM networks n1 WHERE n1.network <<= (SELECT n2.network FROM networks n2 WHERE n2.id = 1);
CREATE TABLE user_networks (user_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, network INET);
INSERT INTO user_networks (user_id, network) VALUES (1, inet '192.168.1.10/24');
SELECT ul.network FROM user_networks ul WHERE ul.network <<= (SELECT n.network FROM networks n WHERE n.id = 1);
SELECT n.network FROM networks n WHERE n.network <<= inet '192.168.1/24' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM user_networks u WHERE u.network <<= n.network);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM networks n1 WHERE n1.network <<= (SELECT n2.network FROM networks n2 WHERE n2.id = 2);
SELECT DISTINCT n1.network FROM networks n1 JOIN user_networks u ON u.network <<= n1.network WHERE u.user_id = 1;
SELECT n.network FROM networks n JOIN subnetworks s ON s.subnet <<= n.network WHERE s.subnet = inet '192.168.1.0/24';
